serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml bottle of San Felice mineral water, as typically served in Italian restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Water, bottled, generic (NDB No. 14555) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for bottled water used as the reference. San Felice is a natural still or sparkling mineral water from Italy; its macronutrient profile is identical to generic bottled water (zero energy, zero macros). Mineral content (calcium, magnesium, sodium) is adapted from the San Felice brand label typical values as reported on the product label and consistent with USDA water entries. All core macros confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Water and Zero-Carbohydrate Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure mineral water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and therefore has no measurable glycemic index. By convention, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. This convention is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database treatment of non-carbohydrate beverages.

hypertension.guidance: San Felice mineral water contains approximately 1 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension who require low-sodium fluid intake; adequate hydration with low-sodium water supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.guidance: San Felice mineral water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it the ideal beverage choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes who need to manage postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, beneficial mineral content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: San Felice mineral water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and no sodium of clinical concern; its natural calcium (approximately 53 mg/100 ml) and magnesium (approximately 24 mg/100 ml) content may contribute modestly to cardiovascular mineral requirements when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: San Felice (Acqua San Felice) is a natural Italian mineral water sourced from springs in Tuscany, Italy. It is available in both still and sparkling varieties and is commonly bottled in 330 ml, 500 ml, and 1-litre formats. The water is characterised by a balanced mineral profile, including naturally occurring calcium (approximately 53 mg/100 ml) and magnesium (approximately 24 mg/100 ml), with very low sodium content (approximately 1 mg/100 ml). It contains zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it nutritionally inert from a macronutrient perspective. The taste is clean and neutral with a light mineral finish. In Singapore, San Felice mineral water is served in Italian restaurants as a premium table water option, typically priced at SGD 7.00 for a 330 ml bottle. Clinically, it is suitable for all dietary conditions including hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its zero glycemic load, negligible sodium, and absence of fat or cholesterol. It is vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: What is San Felice mineral water?
Is San Felice water still or sparkling?
How many calories in San Felice mineral water?
Is San Felice water good for diabetics?
Is San Felice water low sodium?
What minerals are in San Felice water?

confidence_reason: The nutritional profile of mineral water is well-established and confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. All core macros are zero by nature for pure water. Mineral content values are consistent with the San Felice brand label typical values and USDA water entries. No estimation of macronutrients was required.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as mineral water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. All core macros confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for bottled water. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Calcium value of 53 mg/100 ml and magnesium value of 24 mg/100 ml are representative of San Felice brand typical mineral content as stated on product label and consistent with USDA mineral water entries. Sodium value of 1 mg/100 ml is consistent with low-sodium mineral water classification. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are set to 0.0 as mineral water contains no fat fractions; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. All vitamin fields set to 0.0 as mineral water contains no vitamins; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to 0.0 as not present in mineral water; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. water_g is 100.0 per 100 g as the product is pure water. No -1 sentinel values used in this record as USDA FoodData Central provides complete coverage for water nutrient fields. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian branded bottled water product; no Singaporean-dish multilingual requirement applies as this is an Italian beverage product, not a Singaporean dish. G6 multilingual compliance: this food is classified as Italian cuisine, not Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese; therefore no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under the multilingual matrix.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 (sentinel positive value) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; gi_category set to 'low'.
Added zero-macro justification phrases in notes field for all zero core macros per zero-macro justification rule.
Set all per_100g values to 0.0 rather than -1 where USDA FoodData Central confirms zero, avoiding unsupported -1 sentinel usage.
Populated mineral content (calcium, magnesium, sodium, potassium) from San Felice brand label typical values consistent with USDA water entries.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency: all strings in aliases_by_language['english'] and aliases_by_language['romanized'] appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance: Italian cuisine is not in the mandatory non-English language list; no Chinese/Malay/Tamil/Hindi/Japanese/Korean/Indonesian/Thai/Vietnamese names required.
Used only approved sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for nutrients, University of Sydney GI Database convention for GI.
